title: Text & control contrast meets AA
check: Verify body text, link/CTA labels, and functional control boundaries meet WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 normal text, 3:1 large text and non-text/UI boundaries) against their actual backgrounds, including text overlaid on hero imagery.

title: Images have appropriate alt text
check: Verify every content/photographic image (hero, gallery, service photos) carries descriptive alt text and decorative images use alt="" or a presentational role, with no images missing the alt attribute entirely.

title: Form inputs have associated labels
check: Verify every contact/search/newsletter input has a persistent visible label programmatically associated via <label for>/id, aria-label, or aria-labelledby, with no placeholder-only fields.

title: Links and buttons have accessible names
check: Verify every link and button — especially icon-only ones (social icons, click-to-call, map pin, mobile menu toggle, FAQ toggle) — exposes a non-empty accessible name via visible text, aria-label, or alt/title on a child image/SVG.

title: Document language is set and valid
check: Verify the root <html> element carries a non-empty, well-formed BCP-47 lang attribute (e.g. en, en-CA).

title: Visible keyboard focus indicators
check: Verify every focusable control (nav links, CTAs, FAQ summaries, menu toggle, form fields) shows a perceivable focus indicator and the default outline is not removed without a replacement.

title: Keyboard operable, no traps
check: Verify a keyboard-only walkthrough (Tab/Shift-Tab/Enter/Space/Esc) reaches and operates every interactive element including the collapsed mobile nav, with logical focus order, no traps, and the sticky header not obscuring focused targets.

title: Adequate tap-target sizing
check: Verify interactive targets (click-to-call, booking/CTA buttons, nav toggle, FAQ summaries, map link) are at least 24x24 CSS px or have 24px of clear spacing, with ~44px recommended for primary mobile actions.

title: Reflow & text resize
check: Verify the page reflows to a single column without two-dimensional scrolling at a 320px viewport (400% zoom), text resizes to 200% without loss of content, and zoom is not suppressed in the viewport meta.

title: Logical heading outline
check: Verify the page has exactly one H1 and a heading hierarchy that descends without skipping levels, and a non-empty document <title>.

title: Semantic structure for relationships & forms
check: Verify content relationships are programmatically conveyed — hours expose day/value pairing via table or dl, form fields use correct autocomplete tokens, and validation errors are identified in text and announced (not color/border-only).

title: Respect reduced-motion preference
check: Verify hover/scale/transition animations are gated behind @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce).

title: Automated accessibility baseline
check: Capture the PSI/Lighthouse accessibility score as a coverage baseline, treating it as the automatable ~13-57% subset only — a high score is not WCAG AA conformance and must be paired with the manual checks above.

title: Honest accessibility claim & legal framing
check: Verify reported a11y findings use defensible build-time/best-practice language ('passes automated checks plus a manual keyboard + screen-reader pass') rather than guaranteed 'WCAG AA compliant', and frame value as reducing Alberta Human Rights Act exposure — not legal compliance.

title: Price signal at the right granularity
check: Verify the site publishes a price signal matching its vertical's correct granularity (barber/cafe/spa = fixed itemized prices; trades/auto = ranges or 'starting at' + estimate CTA; auto also labor rate; dental = ranges for high-ticket elective; law = billing-model + optional fixed fee; retail = per-item when transactional) and never ships zero price signal nor a hard fixed price on site-dependent work.

title: Sufficient real body copy
check: Verify the page carries roughly 600-1,200 words of real body copy across hero/about/services/FAQ/area (homepage body ≥300-500 words) so it reads as credible and rankable rather than a thin brochure.

title: Per-service descriptive copy
check: Verify each service (or menu item) has real descriptive copy answering buyer questions, not a bare price-only list, and that owners' raw list has been expanded into selling prose.

title: About / owner-story block
check: Verify a real About/owner-story block names the human(s) behind the business and pairs with a real photo as an E-E-A-T experience and trust signal.

title: Localized service-area copy
check: Verify neighborhood/service-area copy names the actual Calgary areas served with genuine local context rather than a generic city-name swap, and that multi-location sites use uniquely-authored per-location pages.

title: FAQ with real questions
check: Verify an FAQ block answers real, specific pre-visit questions (parking, walk-in vs appointment, payment/insurance, what to bring) for on-page conversion, voice and AI-search citation.

title: Quoted reviews / testimonials
check: Verify real reviews are surfaced as on-page quoted copy (actual words, not just a star number), serving as the top local trust signal and keyword-rich content.

title: Credentials, licenses & association badges
check: Verify trust/credential copy is present and vertical-appropriate (auto AMVIC licence + all-in pricing + written-estimate/warranty messaging; trades licensed/bonded/WCB/insured; law/dental compliant non-superlative claims; spa association membership; relevant accreditation badges) without compliance-violating superlatives or outcome guarantees.

title: Lawful marketing claims
check: Verify copy complies with the federal Competition Act and Alberta professional rules: testimonials genuine, no unproven performance guarantees, no drip pricing, and no 'specialist'/'best'/'top' superlatives or settlement-amount/outcome claims for regulated law and dental.

title: Authentic real photography
check: Verify the site uses genuine business-specific photography (owner/staff, premises/storefront, work/results) over stock, meeting the minimum viable set of a storefront shot, an owner/team photo, and an interior/results shot.

title: Vertical-specific results / product imagery
check: Verify the conversion-driving visual for the vertical is present (trades before/after project galleries with city+scope captions; cafe real food photos of best-sellers; barber/spa results gallery + atmosphere; retail real product shots; fitness facility/member imagery), with required Alberta PIPA consent for any client before/after images.

title: Cafe menu item detail
check: Verify the cafe menu renders in-page (not PDF) with name + fixed price per item, descriptions, and photos/dietary tags on best-sellers, plus MenuItem schema.

title: Retail/service item detail & schema
check: Verify priced items carry the right item-level fields and schema (retail: name+price+image+Offer/availability/condition, unique description, alt text; spa/barber: name+price+duration+description; auto: honest ranges + turnaround) with catalogue depth capped to the conversion-relevant subset.

title: Consistent NAP from one source
check: Verify NAP is byte-for-byte consistent across page body, footer, LocalBusiness JSON-LD, and GBP, derived from one canonical source, with no call-tracking number breaking the crawled HTML (minor abbreviation differences ignored).

title: Accurate, machine-readable hours
check: Verify opening hours are published and accurate, rendered both as a human-readable table and as openingHoursSpecification JSON-LD (hh:mm:ss, per-day) that agree, with holiday/special hours authored as dated exceptions.

title: Correct location vs service-area framing
check: Verify storefront verticals frame as 'located at Y' (full address + map + PostalAddress JSON-LD) while mobile/service-area verticals frame as 'serves area X' (named cities + areaServed JSON-LD), with the GBP address-hiding visibility risk flagged for SAB clients.

title: Generated SEO/accessibility micro-content
check: Verify image alt text, meta descriptions, and per-page title tags are present and populated, and that AI-generated content lands in the client's owned CMS as the live editable default.

title: Phone number is a real tel: click-to-call link
check: Verify the business phone is an href="tel:+1..." anchor in E.164 format (not plain text or image) with a visible number plus icon, so a single mobile tap dials.

title: One dominant primary CTA above the fold
check: Verify exactly one visually dominant, high-contrast primary CTA sits above the fold in the hero/nav, with any secondary actions visually subordinate (ghost/text) rather than equal-weight competing buttons.

title: Primary CTA matches the vertical's macro-conversion
check: Verify the dominant CTA is the right type for the vertical: book for barber/spa/fitness/dental, click-to-call for trades/law (with quote/consult secondary), book-with-call for auto, order/reserve/view-menu for cafe, directions/shop for retail, free-trial for fitness.

title: Persistent sticky mobile CTA in the thumb zone
check: Verify a sticky/persistent CTA (bottom bar or sticky-nav button) echoes the primary action and stays reachable at every scroll depth on mobile, placed in the lower thumb zone with a >=44px high-contrast tap target.

title: Primary CTA repeated through the page with specific copy
check: Verify the primary action is echoed in nav, hero, and a closing CTA section (phone-first verticals also repeat the number in header/footer) and that CTA copy is action/outcome-specific per vertical, not generic 'Contact Us'/'Submit'.

title: Booking/order flow embedded on-domain, not an off-site redirect
check: Verify the booking/order/reservation entry point is embedded inline (or a pop-up/modal) on the shop's own page and intent is collected on-site before any vendor hand-off, rather than redirecting off-site or using a mobile-broken raw iframe.

title: Low-friction guest booking flow
check: Verify the booking path is a short service->time->confirm guest flow (no forced account creation before commitment, minimal pre-commit fields) with prices/durations visible next to the Book CTA.

title: Booking widget paired with a parallel click-to-call path
check: Verify appointment verticals expose both a prominent booking link and a tel: click-to-call as parallel paths (book primary, call secondary for barber/spa/dental; call primary, book secondary for trades/auto) rather than only one.

title: Contact form is short with phone optional
check: Verify the public contact form ships ~3 fields (Name, Email, Message) with phone optional (never required), defers long qualifying/intake questions to follow-up, and uses multi-step only if it must exceed ~5 fields.

title: Form is single-column with correct mobile input UX
check: Verify the form is single-column with top-aligned labels (no placeholder-only labels), correct type/inputmode/autocomplete on email/phone/name, on-blur inline validation, and a hidden honeypot instead of a CAPTCHA.

title: Form submits successfully and confirms next steps
check: Verify the form actually submits on mobile and returns a clear confirmation that sets expectations (response time) and re-exposes click-to-call, rather than failing silently or collapsing to a bare 'thanks'.

title: Trust cues placed adjacent to the primary CTA
check: Verify a compact star rating + review count and a named testimonial sit near the hero/closing CTA (and hours+NAP are co-located with the action), following a 2-3 cue hierarchy rather than isolated on a separate page or only in the footer.

title: Vertical-specific trust badges and real photography
check: Verify vertical-appropriate proof appears near the CTA (licensed/bonded/insured + warranty for trades; ASE/warranty for auto; credentials/before-after for dental/spa; Super Lawyers/bar/case-results for law; real work gallery for barber/spa/fitness) and that owner/staff/work photos are real, not stock, with local/years-in-business proof in Stats.

title: Mobile-responsive, fast, and HTTPS-secure at the conversion point
check: Verify the site is mobile-responsive and loads fast (so CTAs are usable), serves over HTTPS with security/privacy reassurance at the form, and publishes menu/price lists as HTML rather than a pinch-zoom PDF.

title: Crawlable NAP as real text
check: Verify the business name, full street address, and phone appear as crawlable HTML text (footer + contact), not baked into images, so a single canonical NAP is machine-readable.

title: Click-to-call with text phone
check: Verify a tel: click-to-call link exists AND the same phone number is also rendered as crawlable text (not only in the href or an image) so it contributes to NAP entity resolution.

title: NAP matches GBP & citations
check: Judge whether the site's rendered NAP plausibly matches the Google Business Profile and major directories exactly; flag any internal inconsistency the site reveals (this is foundational table-stakes, not a primary rank lever).

title: LocalBusiness JSON-LD present
check: Verify a LocalBusiness JSON-LD block exists with at minimum the two required properties name and a structured PostalAddress (streetAddress, addressLocality, addressRegion, postalCode, addressCountry).

title: Most-specific @type subtype
check: Verify the JSON-LD uses the most specific schema.org subtype for the vertical (e.g. HairSalon, CafeOrCoffeeShop, DaySpa, Dentist, AutoRepair, LegalService, Plumber, HealthClub, Store) rather than generic LocalBusiness.

title: Rich JSON-LD local fields
check: Verify the JSON-LD goes beyond a name+address stub to include geo (GeoCoordinates >=5 decimals), telephone in +1-403 international format, url, and structured openingHoursSpecification (and priceRange where meaningful).

title: No self-serving review schema
check: Verify the LocalBusiness/Organization JSON-LD does NOT emit self-applied aggregateRating or review about the business itself (policy violation; ineligible for star results and risks a spammy-structured-data penalty) — treat its presence as a defect, not a credit.

title: Machine-readable hours
check: Verify opening hours are encoded as structured openingHoursSpecification objects (dayOfWeek/opens/closes) powering 'open now' answers, not just a visual table or an image of hours.

title: On-page service+city targeting
check: Verify title tags and H1/H2 headings include the service plus Calgary geo-targeting (quadrant NW/NE/SW/SE or named community) rather than generic 'near me' phrasing — the website's largest lever is local organic, not the 3-pack.

title: Dedicated service/location pages
check: Verify the IA matches the business: dedicated service pages where offerings are core, unique per-city service-area pages for SABs serving multiple cities, and one location page (own NAP + schema) per physical location — not a thin single page or duplicate doorway pages.

title: Service-area posture (SAB vs storefront)
check: For service-area businesses (trades, mobile auto) verify areaServed/service-area copy and schema with the public street address omitted; for storefronts verify a visible address + geo is the primary signal and areaServed is not misused.

title: Crawlable menu / FoodEstablishment fields
check: For cafe/restaurant verify a crawlable HTML menu (hasMenu URL or Menu/MenuSection/MenuItem markup, not a PDF-only menu) plus food fields like servesCuisine/acceptsReservations; for other service verticals verify the service list is crawlable HTML, not a PDF.

title: Embedded map & profile links
check: Verify an embedded interactive Google Map (iframe/maps link) and hasMap/sameAs links to authoritative profiles (GBP/Maps, Facebook, Instagram, Yelp) reinforce location and entity reconciliation — a static map image alone does not count.

title: Live reviews display + review CTA
check: Verify the site displays fresh social proof (preferably an async/lazy live GBP/third-party widget with reserved height, not stale static testimonials) and includes a direct Google review CTA / short link the owner can drive — without implying on-site star rich results.

title: Booking / appointment CTA
check: Verify a conversion-complete booking/order/quote CTA appropriate to the vertical (Reserve-with-Google native button for cafe/dental/fitness where supported, GBP appointment link for barber/spa/auto, quote/request form for auto/trades) linking the right Canada-available provider.

title: Fast mobile-first conversion shell
check: Verify the site is fast and mobile-first (good PSI SEO/Core Web Vitals, click-to-call, booking) and English-only is appropriate for Calgary, so it converts GBP-sent traffic and feeds behavioral/AI-discovery signals.

title: Canonical citation source (no volume program)
check: Judge that the site is the clean canonical NAP source for the Canadian tier-1 citation core (GBP, Bing, Apple, Yelp.ca, Facebook, YellowPages.ca, Canada411) and 2-4 relevant vertical directories — without selling an ongoing high-volume citation retainer.

title: Field-first grading with lab fallback
check: Auditor prefers CrUX field p75 (URL-level, then origin-level) for the pass/fail grade and falls back to lab metrics only when field data is absent, labeling that case 'lab-only / no real-user data' rather than a blank or failed CWV section.

title: Record field-data availability state
check: Auditor records which of three field-data states applies (URL-level, origin-level only, or none) and treats missing CrUX data as the expected norm for low-traffic single-location shops rather than a defect, never citing the undisclosed traffic threshold as a Google-stated fact.
signal: psi.cwv.pass (present => field data exists; absent => no field data)

title: Grade on individual CWV metrics at correct thresholds
check: Auditor stores LCP, CLS, and the TBT/INP proxy individually against current good thresholds (LCP<=2.5s, INP<=200ms, CLS<=0.1 at p75) and grades off these rather than the blended Lighthouse 0-100 composite, with FID never appearing.

title: Do not hard-grade INP in lab mode
check: Auditor never reports a hard INP pass/fail from Lighthouse alone, labels INP as 'lab-estimated / not measurable in lab' (TBT as proxy), and does not penalize brochure-style shop sites on INP/TBT unless real CrUX field INP exists.
signal: psi.cwv.inp (field) present vs absent; psi.performance (TBT-derived) for lab-only

title: Treat lab metrics as conservative worst-case
check: When grading on lab data, auditor treats it as a pessimistic worst-case floor (Slow-4G + CPU throttle + cold cache, not the Calgary median), pins which tool/throttling was used (PSI ~1.2x vs local Lighthouse 4x), and does not hard-fail a page on lab LCP/CLS alone when no field data exists.
signal: psi.performance; psi.cwv.lcp; psi.cwv.cls (lab values when field absent)

title: Use medianed lab runs, no knife-edge grading
check: Auditor uses a median of multiple lab passes (or PSI's internal median) and does not grade pass/fail on a single run sitting within ~+/-5-10 points of a threshold.

title: LCP is the dominant mobile failure
check: Auditor prioritizes mobile LCP as the most-failed and most load-bearing perf metric, recognizing the LCP element is almost always the hero image/main heading and that resource load delay (late-discovered hero) hurts more than raw image bytes.

title: Hero image format, sizing, and weight
check: Auditor verifies the hero/LCP image is served in a modern format (AVIF/WebP) with correctly sized responsive variants and reasonable weight, not an oversized JPEG/PNG.

title: Hero loaded eagerly with high priority
check: Auditor verifies the hero/LCP image is an eager in the initial HTML (never loading='lazy') and ideally preloaded or marked fetchpriority='high', with lazy-loading reserved for below-the-fold images only.

title: Explicit dimensions on images and embeds
check: Auditor verifies every and

title: Web-font loading discipline
check: Auditor verifies fonts use font-display:swap (or optional) with size-matched fallbacks and are self-hosted/preloaded rather than render-blocking Google Fonts links, mitigating both font-driven CLS and text-LCP delay.

title: Platform / page-builder bloat
check: Auditor identifies the site's platform and flags WordPress + page-builder (Elementor/Divi) builds shipping multi-MB pages as the strongest single predictor of a failing mobile CWV score.

title: Third-party embeds controlled
check: Auditor checks that booking/map/chat embeds and other third-party widgets are deliberately controlled (facade/link-out, or lazy-loaded with reserved space) rather than eagerly loading heavy iframe JS that competes for bandwidth and blocks the main thread.

title: Static delivery from a CDN / low TTFB
check: Auditor checks the site serves pre-rendered HTML from a CDN/edge host (low TTFB) rather than a slow shared origin with no CDN, since high TTFB is the largest poor-LCP subpart.

title: Do not penalize non-scoring advisories
check: Auditor does not deduct for Lighthouse Opportunities/Diagnostics that do not bear on the score (e.g. 'Remove unused CSS', 'efficient cache policy'), and ignores cache-policy flags on third-party cross-origin assets the owner cannot control.

title: Honest ranking / business-impact framing
check: Auditor frames CWV as a UX/conversion quality floor (faster mobile load reduces bounce and lifts conversions) and not as a local-pack ranking lever, since proximity/GBP/reviews dominate local ranking and Google says good CWV does not guarantee ranking.

title: Served over HTTPS
check: Verify the site's final resolved URL is https:// so the browser does not show a 'Not Secure' label and visitors are not blocked by future HTTPS-First interstitials.

title: HTTP upgrades to HTTPS
check: Fetch the http:// URL and confirm it 301-redirects to https:// with no plaintext step in the chain, so the insecure version of the site is not left reachable.

title: Valid, matching TLS certificate
check: Confirm the certificate is not expired, self-signed, or hostname-mismatched (and that www/non-www both resolve safely) so visitors never hit a full-page 'Your connection is not private' block.

title: Form and booking endpoints use HTTPS
check: Verify any contact-form action URL and any outbound booking link target HTTPS endpoints, since an insecure form action triggers Chrome's autofill-disable and submission-block warning.

title: HSTS header present
check: Check whether a Strict-Transport-Security response header is sent; a low-severity, non-visitor-facing hardening item recorded but never used as an outreach headline.

title: Any social proof visible
check: Verify the page surfaces at least one social-proof element (star rating, review count, or testimonial), the baseline trust floor for a local business.

title: Visible star rating with review volume
check: Verify a numeric star rating (e.g. '4.8 stars') is shown alongside a review count (e.g. 'based on 240 reviews') so visitors can pass their threshold and volume checks on-page.

title: Named, photo-attributed testimonials
check: Verify testimonials carry a real-name byline (and ideally a customer photo) rather than anonymous quotes, which read as fabricated.

title: Live/linked third-party reviews
check: Verify the page embeds or links through to a verifiable third-party review source (Google Business Profile / Yelp), which is publicly checkable and always-fresh versus static quotes.

title: AggregateRating/Review structured data
check: Detect AggregateRating or Review JSON-LD/microdata, an invisible signal whose payoff is review-star rich snippets and higher organic CTR.

title: Third-party badges recorded, not credited
check: Record presence of generic static seals (BBB, security/payment logos) and Google Verified/Guarantee badges, but treat generic badges as neutral-to-risky rather than a positive trust signal.

title: Privacy policy linked
check: Scan the DOM for an anchor whose href or text matches a privacy-policy pattern; absence is a common small-business gap and a strong outreach hook.

title: Form/booking present but no privacy policy (PIPA gap)
check: Flag when a contact form or booking link is detected but no privacy policy is linked, since collecting PII via US-hosted endpoints with no policy is a documented PIPA non-compliance finding.

title: PIPA content flags for manual review
check: Flag for manual review whether the privacy policy includes a near-form purpose notice, a named privacy officer, and disclosure of named US/offshore service providers, none reliably auto-detectable.

title: Cookie notice and form spam protection
check: Detect a cookie/consent notice and anti-bot protection on the contact form (Turnstile/reCAPTCHA/honeypot), the lighter PIPA safeguards posture distinct from the privacy policy itself.

title: NAP, hours, and map present
check: Verify consistent name/address/phone, click-to-call, accurate opening hours, and a map are present on the page, the baseline legitimacy signals consumers and the Local Pack rely on.

title: LocalBusiness JSON-LD
check: Detect LocalBusiness structured data (name+address), an invisible-but-read-by-Google identity/trust signal whose absence is a silent gap.

title: About/owner identity surface
check: Verify a genuine About/identity surface exists with named owner/team, real (non-stock) photography, and optionally years-in-business, all recognized E-E-A-T trust signals.

title: Professional credentials (YMYL/licensed verticals)
check: For licensed/risk-bearing verticals, verify display of credentials (licensed/bonded/insured/WCB, bar/college registration, association badges), a high-impact hiring-confidence cue.

title: Correct LocalBusiness schema subtype per vertical
check: Verify the JSON-LD emits the most specific LocalBusiness subtype for the vertical (HairSalon, CafeOrCoffeeShop, DaySpa/BeautySalon, ExerciseGym/HealthClub, Dentist, Attorney/LegalService, AutoRepair, Store, or a HomeAndConstructionBusiness subtype) rather than generic LocalBusiness.

title: Cafe menu markup and per-item food photography
check: Verify the cafe site exposes a structured Menu (hasMenu/hasMenuSection/hasMenuItem + servesCuisine) and an on-page menu with per-item food photos rather than text-only.

title: Services modeled as Service/OfferCatalog with area/specialty props
check: Verify service businesses model their offerings as Service + makesOffer/OfferCatalog (not Product) and emit the vertical-relevant recommended props: priceRange (all), areaServed (trades/auto/law), medicalSpecialty (dental).

title: Dental new-patient appointment + intake conversion path
check: Verify the dental site leads with a new-patient appointment-request CTA plus click-to-call, and provides the supporting anatomy (services, new-patient/insurance info, intake form with health-history + insurance upload, team bios, FAQ).

title: Dental regulatory registration, insurance/direct-billing, consented before/after
check: Verify the dental site displays ADA&C/CDSA registration (truthful, no uncertified specialty claims), lists accepted insurance/direct-billing, and any before/after gallery is consent-gated.

title: Law single consultation/case-evaluation CTA with short intake
check: Verify the law-firm site has one specific consultation/case-evaluation CTA (not generic 'Contact Us') repeated in sticky header and mid-page, a short essential-fields intake form, and prominent click-to-call, with no e-commerce.

title: Law per-case-type practice-area pages with targeted CTA
check: Verify the law-firm site has dedicated practice-area pages (e.g. personal injury, immigration, family) each with empathetic client-language messaging and its own intake/contact CTA, not a single broad page.

title: Law Society credential, prohibited-language and results disclaimer
check: Verify the law-firm site shows Law Society of Alberta membership and attorney bar credentials, avoids prohibited terms ('specialist','expert','best','top','leading', advertised settlement amounts), and includes a past-results disclaimer where outcomes are referenced.

title: Auto dual conversion: schedule-service + quote/estimate request
check: Verify the auto repair site has a dominant 'Schedule Service' CTA plus a quote/estimate request mechanism, backed by sticky click-to-call, and the homepage answers can-you-fix-it / can-I-trust-you / how-do-I-book.

title: Auto AMVIC licence, certifications, warranty and pricing
check: Verify the auto repair site displays its AMVIC licence number, technician/ASE/Red Seal certifications and warranty/financing, plus a transparent service menu with pricing and real shop photos.

title: Fitness free-trial CTA + embedded bookable class timetable
check: Verify the fitness site leads with a free-trial/intro-offer CTA and exposes an embedded, bookable class schedule/timetable plus membership/pricing packages, trainer profiles and waiver-at-booking.

title: Trades WCB coverage, licensed-and-insured, quote request
check: Verify the trades site shows WCB Alberta coverage (clearance/number), a 'licensed & insured' statement, and provides a quote/estimate request mechanism with areaServed/'serving [area]' framing.

title: Spa massage/RMT practitioner and facility credentials
check: Verify a massage-offering spa displays its Calgary massage-practitioner/RMT credential and AHS facility approval; for esthetics-only services, voluntary certifications are surfaced honestly (not licensed in Alberta).

title: Embedded booking widget for appointment-driven verticals
check: Verify appointment-driven verticals provide a real booking path (embedded widget or hosted booking link, e.g. Jane App/NexHealth/Mindbody/Reserve-with-Google), not just a phone number.

title: Governing-credential badges surfaced, limited to a few
check: Verify regulated verticals surface their governing licence/credential as a trust badge while keeping the badge count to ~3-5 relevant logos, not a wall of irrelevant badges.

title: Vertical-appropriate visual-proof galleries and team bios
check: Verify each vertical ships its conversion-critical visual proof: before/after gallery (spa/dental/auto/barber), categorized project/job gallery with location captions (trades/auto), team headshot bios (dental/law/fitness/barber/spa), and real (non-stock) photography throughout.

title: Clean, credible first impression
check: Auditor verifies the homepage reads as professionally designed and modern at a glance (the ~50ms verdict), clearing the table-stakes 'looks legitimate, not amateur or dated' bar.

title: Real, authentic photography over stock
check: Auditor verifies imagery shows the actual shop, staff, location, products, or work rather than generic staged stock photos or clip-art.

title: High-resolution, undistorted images
check: Auditor verifies images are sharp and correctly proportioned, with no low-resolution, blurry, dark, pixelated, upscaled, or stretched/wrong-aspect-ratio imagery.

title: Strong hero / visual masthead
check: Auditor verifies an above-the-fold hero photo or strong brand visual carries the first impression, rather than flat blocks with default fonts.

title: Intentional type pairing, no default/novelty fonts
check: Auditor verifies the site uses a deliberate display + body typeface pairing (web fonts declared) rather than bare browser-default Times/Arial or novelty fonts (Comic Sans, Papyrus), and keeps to 2-3 typefaces.

title: Readable body text (size, line-height, measure)
check: Auditor verifies body copy is at least 16px with line-height >= 1.5 and line length in the ~45-75 character range, not tiny, tightly-leaded, or full-bleed text.
signal: computed font-size of body/paragraph text >= 16px, line-height >= 1.5, and text column max-width ~60-75ch

title: WCAG AA color contrast
check: Auditor verifies text meets WCAG AA contrast (>= 4.5:1 normal, >= 3:1 large), flagging low-contrast grey-on-white body copy.
signal: computed contrast ratio of text vs background; flag body text < 4.5:1, large text < 3:1 (axe-core / contrast engine)

title: Clear visual hierarchy and scannable content
check: Auditor verifies a consistent modular type scale gives distinct heading vs body sizing, an emphasized headline and standout CTA, and content is chunked rather than a wall of text.

title: Generous whitespace, low clutter
check: Auditor verifies the layout has generous breathing room and low visual complexity rather than a cramped, cluttered, high-density layout.

title: Cohesive, restrained color palette
check: Auditor verifies a coherent vertical-appropriate palette (~2 neutrals + 1 accent) rather than many clashing, rainbow, or washed-out colors, and consistent brand color across the site.

title: No dated era-markers or DIY chrome
check: Auditor verifies the design is modern, flagging skeuomorphic styling (beveled/glossy buttons, heavy shadows, faux textures, busy gradients), autoplay hero carousels, builder watermarks ('Powered by Wix'), and unmodified default-template chrome.

title: Responsive mobile layout and tap targets
check: Auditor verifies a mobile viewport renders without horizontal scroll, overflow, or tiny text, and interactive elements (especially call/booking CTAs) meet ~44px tap-target minimums.

title: No typos, broken, or placeholder elements
check: Auditor verifies the homepage is free of visible typos, Lorem ipsum, 'image coming soon', broken-image glyphs, dead/placeholder links, and stale content (old copyright year, off-season banners, expired promos).

title: No intrusive pop-ups or autoplay audio
check: Auditor verifies the page does not block the hero with an immediate pop-up/interstitial/cookie wall or play audio/video on load, and that any motion respects prefers-reduced-motion.

title: Design tier matches business price point and vertical
check: Auditor verifies the visual polish fits the shop's price point and vertical register (premium/aesthetic services read calm and refined; trades/auto read clear and trustworthy), flagging a cheap/templated look on a premium business or over-luxe design on a budget shop.

title: Consistent branding and present logo/favicon
check: Auditor verifies uniform colors, fonts, spacing, and tone across the site, a present logo, and a custom favicon (a missing favicon is a flagged credibility gap).

title: Per-vertical visual differentiation
check: Auditor verifies the look feels appropriate to its vertical (a barber feels different from a cafe from a law office) through color, type, radius, and imagery rather than generic sameness.
